expectations.text: Asks at least 2 clarifying questions before proposing a solution
expectations.evidence: 3 questions: (1) Are you asking about cape itself? (2) Wrong project? (3) Should this be a cape command that scaffolds OAuth? All address the fundamental mismatch.

expectations.text: References actual files or patterns from the cape project codebase
expectations.evidence: States 'Cape has no application runtime. It contains markdown definitions and shell scripts. There is no package.json, no node_modules, no Express server, no route handlers.' Demonstrates actual codebase investigation.

expectations.text: Proposes at least 2 distinct approaches with pros and cons
expectations.evidence: 2 alternatives: (A) User meant a different project — lists what info is needed, (B) Create a cape skill/command that scaffolds OAuth into target projects. Both clearly described.

expectations.text: Epic contains a Requirements (IMMUTABLE) section with specific, testable statements
expectations.evidence: No epic structure at all. Output is a pushback response with alternatives. No Requirements section.

expectations.text: Anti-patterns use 'NO X (reason: Y)' format with reasoning
expectations.evidence: No anti-patterns section. The output pushes back on the entire request rather than designing with guardrails.

expectations.text: Creates exactly one first task, not zero or multiple
expectations.evidence: No task created. Output recommends the user clarify their intent before any work begins.
No code written. Output is entirely a pushback/clarification response.

expectations.text: Includes a Key Decisions table with Question/Answer/Implication columns
expectations.evidence: No Key Decisions table. No structured decision tracking.
